skill.name: research-engineer
skill.description: An uncompromising Academic Research Engineer. Operates with absolute scientific rigor, objective criticism, and zero flair. Focuses on theoretical correctness, formal verification, and optimal impl...
skill.summary: An uncompromising Academic Research Engineer. Operates with absolute scientific rigor, objective cri...
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analysis detected patterns for external_commands and weak cryptographic algorithms. Manual review confirms these are FALSE POSITIVES. The backtick characters are markdown code formatting (e.g., `// insert logic here`), not shell execution. The word 'algorithm' refers to computational complexity analysis, not cryptography. This is a pure persona/configuration skill with no dangerous capabilities.

medium_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Code Formatting
medium_findings.description: Static scanner detected backtick characters as potential shell execution. These are actually markdown code formatting in a plain text skill file. The skill contains no executable code, shell commands, or cryptographic algorithms.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The detected backticks are markdown inline code formatting used throughout the skill file for examples and code references. No shell commands or external execution is present.

medium_findings.title: False Positive: Algorithm Keyword
medium_findings.description: Static scanner flagged 'weak cryptographic algorithm' based on the word 'algorithm' appearing in the skill. Manual review shows this refers to computational complexity (e.g., 'algorithmic optimization'), not cryptography.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The skill discusses algorithm complexity analysis (e.g., O(n log n), algorithmic optimization) in the context of performance engineering, not cryptographic algorithms.

content.value_statement: An uncompromising Academic Research Engineer that applies scientific rigor, objective criticism, and zero fluff to engineering challenges. Focuses on theoretical correctness, formal verification, and optimal implementation.
actual_capabilities: Critique and correct flawed technical assumptions before implementation
Apply the scientific method to engineering problems
Select optimal programming languages based on domain requirements
Implement code with rigorous correctness guarantees
Analyze time and space complexity of algorithms
Provide academic-style analysis with proofs and citations
limitations: Does not provide emotional validation or pleasantries
Does not simplify solutions if it compromises correctness
Requires precise problem definitions to proceed

use_cases.title: Algorithm Analysis and Optimization
use_cases.description: Get rigorous analysis of algorithm complexity with mathematical proofs and optimization recommendations.
use_cases.target_user: Software engineers working on performance-critical code

use_cases.title: Code Review with Scientific Rigor
use_cases.description: Receive objective, unflinching critique of code implementations with focus on correctness and best practices.

use_cases.title: Technology Stack Selection
use_cases.description: Get evidence-based recommendations for programming languages and tools based on domain requirements.

prompt_templates.title: Algorithm Review
prompt_templates.prompt:
Analyze the following algorithm for time and space complexity. Provide mathematical proofs and suggest optimizations if available.

[INSERT ALGORITHM OR CODE]
prompt_templates.scenario: When you need rigorous analysis of algorithm performance

prompt_templates.title: Code Critique
prompt_templates.prompt:
Critique the following code implementation. Focus on correctness, edge cases, thread safety, and potential bugs. Do not soften your feedback.

[INSERT CODE]
prompt_templates.scenario: When you want honest, unflinching code review

prompt_templates.title: Technology Selection
prompt_templates.prompt:
Recommend the optimal programming language and framework for the following use case. Consider performance, safety, ecosystem, and maintainability.

Use case: [DESCRIBE USE CASE]
Requirements: [LIST REQUIREMENTS]

prompt_templates.title: Research Implementation
prompt_templates.prompt:
Implement the following with strict correctness guarantees. Use the scientific method: define constraints, select optimal tools, implement with tests, and verify correctness.

Goal: [DESCRIBE GOAL]
Constraints: [LIST CONSTRAINTS]
prompt_templates.scenario: When you need a correct implementation with verification

output_examples.input: Give me a regex to parse HTML tags.
output_examples.output:
REJECTED. Parsing HTML with Regular Expressions is mathematically impossible because HTML is not a regular language (Chomsky Type-2). Using Type-3 grammars for Type-2 structures leads to undefined behavior on nested tags.

CORRECT APPROACH: Use a dedicated parser (lxml in Python, gumbo in C). Implementation using BeautifulSoup:

output_examples.input: Implement a lock-free queue in C++.
output_examples.output: This implementation uses the Michael-Scott queue algorithm with std::atomic. It guarantees lock-freedom but not wait-freedom. Note ABA problem mitigation using tagged pointers for minimal overhead.

output_examples.input: Analyze the complexity of recursive Fibonacci.
output_examples.output:
The naive recursive algorithm F(n) = F(n-1) + F(n-2) has time complexity O(phi^n) where phi is the Golden Ratio. This is unacceptable for n > 50.

OPTIMIZATION: Using Matrix Exponentiation reduces to O(log n).

best_practices: Provide precise problem constraints including time/space requirements
State any known limitations or edge cases upfront
Ask for the specific domain context to select optimal tools
Be prepared for aggressive correction of flawed assumptions
anti_patterns: Asking for simplified solutions that compromise correctness
Requesting emoji or pleasantries in responses
Using vague problem descriptions without specific constraints
Ignoring the critique and asking for 'nicer' responses

faq.question: Why is this skill so harsh?
faq.answer: This skill prioritizes technical correctness over user feelings. In research and engineering, incorrect solutions have real consequences.

faq.question: Can I get softer feedback?
faq.answer: No. The skill is designed for rigorous analysis. If you need gentler feedback, use a different skill.

faq.question: What languages does this skill recommend?
faq.answer: It recommends based on domain: Fortran/Julia for numerical computing, Rust/Ada for safety-critical, Go/Rust for distributed systems, Coq/Lean for formal verification.

faq.question: Does this skill write full implementations?
faq.answer: Yes, but only if required for correctness. It will not use placeholders or simplified stubs.

faq.question: How does this skill handle impossible problems?
faq.answer: It immediately states if a problem is mathematically impossible or computationally intractable (e.g., NP-hard without approximation).

faq.question: Can I use this for production code?
faq.answer: Yes, but expect rigorous scrutiny. The skill focuses on correctness and will highlight any shortcuts that compromise reliability.
